key_themes: Weak China sales and disappointing FY2026 revenue guidance (expected low single-digit declines)
Ongoing brand renovation and turnaround execution risks with uncertain timing
Significant insider buying by CEO Elliott Hill (~$1M) and directors (including Timothy Cook) at 52-week lows
Analyst downgrades, neutral ratings, and calls for patience amid prolonged challenges
Deeply discounted valuation with dividend appeal but near-term earnings pressure
summary: Nike (NKE) faces predominantly negative sentiment after its fiscal Q3 2026 results showed flat revenue (~$11.3B) and a weak outlook for further sales declines through calendar 2026, especially a ~20% drop in Greater China. This triggered a sharp post-earnings sell-off, with shares down over 30% YTD to around $46 amid skepticism about the 'renovation' plan, DTC challenges, and competitive pressures. While CEO and director insider purchases signal confidence at current depressed levels and some see long-term value with upcoming global sports events, analysts emphasize patience and near-term risks remain elevated.
sources: 15+ news articles (CNBC, Yahoo Finance, Business Insider, company filings) focused on Q3 2026 earnings and sell-off, 25+ X posts from keyword/semantic searches (March-April 2026) discussing $NKE, earnings, and insider activity
